title: Process: The diagram below shows how electricity is generated in a hydroelectric power st...
description: The diagram below shows how electricity is generated in a hydroelectric power station. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Hydroelectric Power Station

1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ minh họa cách một nhà máy thủy điện hoạt động vào ban ngày và ban đêm.

→ Dạng: process (cyclical process \+ comparison day/night)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • hydroelectric power station

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / explains
  • how → the process by which

Ví dụ:

The diagram illustrates how a hydroelectric power station generates electricity during the day and operates at night.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Quy trình gồm 2 giai đoạn chính:

→ ban ngày (generate electricity)

→ ban đêm (pump water back)

  • Nước di chuyển giữa:

→ high-level reservoir và low-level reservoir

→ Key idea:

  • Đây là chu trình khép kín (cyclical process)
  • Ban ngày tạo điện, ban đêm tái sử dụng nước

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Ban ngày (electricity generation)

  • Ban ngày:

→ intake mở

  • Nước từ high-level reservoir:

→ chảy xuống qua đường ống

  • Sau đó:

→ làm quay reversible turbines

  • Turbines:

→ vận hành generator

  • Kết quả:

→ electricity được truyền qua power lines đến national grid

  • Cuối cùng:

→ nước chảy vào low-level reservoir

BODY 2 – Ban đêm (water pumping)

  • Ban đêm:

→ intake đóng

  • Hệ thống hoạt động ngược lại:

→ turbines hoạt động như máy bơm

  • Nước từ low-level reservoir:

→ được bơm ngược lên high-level reservoir

→ Ý:

  • Chuẩn bị cho chu kỳ tiếp theo

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • reservoir → hồ chứa
  • dam → đập nước
  • intake → cửa lấy nước
  • flow → dòng chảy
  • generate electricity → tạo điện
  • reversible turbines → tua-bin hai chiều
  • pump water back → bơm nước ngược lại
  • national grid → lưới điện quốc gia
  • operate → vận hành

The diagram illustrates how a hydroelectric power station operates, showing the different processes that occur during the day and at night.

Overall, electricity is generated in the daytime by releasing water from a high-level reservoir to drive turbines, whereas at night the process is reversed, with water being pumped back up for reuse.

During the day, water stored behind a dam in a high-level reservoir is released through an intake, which is open at this time. The water flows downhill through a pipe and spins reversible turbines located in the power station. These turbines drive a generator, producing electricity that is transmitted via power lines to the national grid. After passing through the turbines, the water is discharged into a low-level reservoir.

At night, when electricity demand is lower, the intake is closed and the turbines operate in reverse. Using electricity from the grid, they pump water from the low-level reservoir back up to the high-level reservoir through the same pipe. This allows the water to be stored and reused for electricity generation the following day.
